messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following pharmaceutical clinical trial participant information sheet for GDPR compliance:

"This clinical trial is sponsored by PharmaCo and conducted under EU Clinical Trials Regulation. We will collect your name, date of birth, medical history, current medications, and trial-related health measurements. Processing is necessary for scientific research in the public interest and for compliance with clinical trial regulations. Your data may be shared with the regulatory authority (EMA) and trial monitors. Data will be retained for 25 years as required by clinical trial law. Participation is voluntary and you may withdraw at any time; withdrawal does not affect data already collected. Your data subject rights are limited by clinical trial legal obligations but you may contact the trial team with any queries. Our DPO is at dpo@pharmaresearch.eu."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Scientific research in the public interest (Art. 6(1)(e)) and legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)) under EU Clinical Trials Regulation both stated. Appropriate for clinical trial processing.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PASS] Health data correctly identified as special category. Processing in the public interest for scientific research per Art. 9(2)(j) and medical treatment necessity per Art. 9(2)(h) are both applicable and implicitly referenced.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Clinical trial conduct, regulatory reporting, and health measurement analysis are clearly stated.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected (name, DOB, medical history, medications, trial measurements) is proportionate and standard for clinical trial participation.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 25-year retention stated and grounded in clinical trial legal requirements.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Limitation on rights due to clinical trial legal obligations is acknowledged and appropriate. A contact route for queries is provided. The specific rights available and which are limited should be more explicitly stated.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] EMA and trial monitors named as recipients with appropriate regulatory context.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PARTIAL] EMA is EU-based. Trial monitors and sponsors may operate globally — no transfer safeguard information provided.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact at dpo@pharmaresearch.eu disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 15-21 (rights that are available vs limited not individually specified), Art. 46 (international transfer safeguards for global trial monitors not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Specify which data subject rights are available and which are restricted under clinical trial law, and provide the basis for each restriction
- Address international transfer safeguards for any non-EEA trial monitors or co-sponsors
- Add breach notification commitment (72-hour supervisory authority notification)
